title: Malpighiodes leucanthele
taxonomy: Malpighiodes leucanthele is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Malpighiaceae. This plant is a member of the Malpighiaceae family, which includes about 130 genera of flowering plants. The Malpighiaceae family is part of the larger order Malpighiales, which comprises approximately 16 orders of flowering plants. Malpighiaceae plants are primarily found in the tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas. The classification of Malpighiodes leucanthele is further categorized under the kingdom Plantae.

intro: Malpighiodes leucanthele, also known as the White-flowered Malpighia, is a flowering plant species belonging to the Malpighiaceae family, native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Malpighiodes leucanthele include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health issues.
